title: Siege of Coruscant
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Coruscant}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|Battle of Mrisst]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |conc= |next=[[Fall of Generis]]''[[The Last Command]]'' |image=[[File:SiegeOfCoruscant-TLC3.jpg]] |name=Siege of Coruscant |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] ([[Thrawn campaign]]) |date=[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] {{C|44:8:11}}'' |place=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |result=Indecisive, Coruscant "besieged" |side1=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |side2=[[Thrawn's confederation]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]] *[[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] *[[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] |commanders2=*[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[New Republic First Fleet|Home Guard Fleet]] *2 [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun]] platforms *[[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] **''[[Harrier]]'' *[[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigate]] **''[[Evanrue]]'' *At least 2 [[Assault Frigate Mark I|Assault Frigates Mk. I]] *[[DP20 gunship/Legends|DP20 Corellian gunships]] *[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] *[[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]] *[[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|v-150 Planet Defender]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] *[[Steadfast (carrier)|''Steadfast'']]''[[Before the Storm]]'' |forces2=*6 [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] **[[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] **''[[Death's Head]]'' *2 [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] *8 [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] *10 [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|''Interdictor''-class heavy cruisers]] *[[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] *22 [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] fitted with [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking devices]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*No notable figures |casual2=*No notable figures |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Symbols are extremely important to the New Republic. That the new government has taken the traditional capital of the Empire, and the Republic before it, is an important symbol of change to the galaxy. The fact that Thrawn could so masterfully manipulate this symbolism to his own advantage underscores the importance of the Siege of Coruscant.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]''}} The '''Siege of Coruscant''' was a major space battle in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] during the [[Thrawn campaign]]. Since his forces were outnumbered by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] defenses of its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] used cloaking technology to create an effective siege weapon. To do this, he fitted twenty-two [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] with cloaking devices and placed them on-board several of his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. ==Prelude== {{Quote|When we're finally ready to draw the Coruscant sector fleet into ambush, Mrisst will be the perfect lure to use. If they come out to meet us, we'll defeat them then and there. And if they somehow sense the trap and refuse to engage, we'll have our forward base. Either way, the Empire will triumph.|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Pellaeon]]|}} Dark Jedi [[Joruus C'baoth]], the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of [[Jorus C'baoth]], insisted that the Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], commander of the Empire at that time, launch a planetary [[Raid on Coruscant (Thrawn Crisis)|attack]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to bring the [[Solo twins]] to him after [[Molo Himron]]'s Imperial commando team failed to capture them. This also served Thrawn's purposes, as the Grand Admiral had been planning an attack into the heart of the New Republic. Despite C'baoth's belief that the Grand Admiral wouldn't do as he had commanded, and the fact that C'baoth took control of the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] and its crew, Thrawn agreed to prepare the attack on Coruscant, after replacing the crew on the ''Chimaera'' injured by C'baoth's taking control with a five hundred man caretaker crew from Captain [[Dorja]]. Thrawn agreed to prepare the fleet for an attack upon Coruscant once his special cargo of asteroids from [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]] had been taken on board the ships of his fleet. Despite having been the one who had insisted upon the attack, C'baoth requested to be taken back to the [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] citadel on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] while the Imperial fleet was mobilizing to attack Coruscant. The ''[[Draklor]]'', under the command of [[Freja Covell]], was detached from the fleet to take C'baoth to Wayland. Shortly after C'baoth left, Thrawn's fleet departed for Coruscant. Prior to the siege, Grand Admiral Thrawn [[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|attacked]] the planet [[Mrisst/Legends|Mrisst]] to distract the [[New Republic First Fleet|Coruscant sector fleet]] away from Coruscant. This strategy left the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] capital world defenseless when Thrawn launched the Siege of Coruscant. ==The battle== {{Quote|Mon Mothma, I hereby request your permission to take command of Coruscant's defenses.''"
"''I would be very grateful if you would do so, Garm.|[[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] and [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]|}} The battle began when ten [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruisers]] and eight [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Katana'' Dreadnaughts]] dropped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] near Coruscant, quickly followed by six [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]]. The Imperial ships were engaged by Coruscant's defense fleet and a pair of [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun|Golan III]] platforms, but New Republic [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]] and his navy, including the [[New Republic First Fleet|Home Guard Fleet]], were thoroughly outmatched by Grand Admiral Thrawn's tactical skill. Thrawn, who was commanding the Imperials from the bridge of his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] the ''Chimaera'', assisted by his Captain, [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], outmatched Drayson in many tactical areas, giving the Imperials an original advantage. New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], observing the battle, recognized the Grand Admiral's favored tactic of using Interdictors to aid in precision [[Precision hyperspace jump|microjumps]] during battle. However, he was unable to warn Drayson before two [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] jumped in to ambush one of the Golan platforms at close range. [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|V-150 Planet Defender]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] were fired uselessly at Thrawn's ship, although these had as much chance of hitting the Golan III battle platforms as they did Thrawn's fleet. Although [[DP20 gunship/Legends|DP20 Corellian gunships]], [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] and [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wings]] were used to engage the Empire, they were unable to stop the plans that Grand Admiral Thrawn was preparing to implement. Shortly afterwards, Bel Iblis took command of Coruscant's forces at the urging of the Head of State of the New Republic, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], as Bel Iblis had previously refused to take command until being approved by Mon Mothma, so as to make sure she trusted him. Bel Iblis pulled back the ships that were defending the Golan platforms, knowing better than Drayson the amount of punishment they could sustain. This effectively gave Thrawn two choices; either he could remain where he was and duel with the battle stations, or he could follow the defenders down to where he would be vulnerable to ground-based weaponry. [[File:StarDestroyerAsteroids.jpg|thumb|left|A Star Destroyer releases asteroids above Coruscant's orbit.]] At this point, Thrawn ordered the ''[[Death's Head]]'' and other ships under his command to place asteroids into low orbit in time with the ''Chimaera'', before ordering the retreat into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], creating an invisible hazard field around the galactic capital. In addition to the twenty-two actual asteroids, Thrawn also staged 265 false launches. As the cloaked asteroids were undetectable to Coruscant's sensors, it was impossible for the Republic to distinguish an actual asteroid launch from a false one. The first asteroid launched collided with the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|escort frigate]] ''[[Evanrue]]''. General Bel Iblis, correctly guessing the nature of the cloaked object, ordered the Dreadnaught ''[[Harrier]]'' to fire at the asteroid's calculated position with its ion cannons. Thrawn allowed the asteroid's cloak to be disabled, revealing that it was indeed an asteroid, before destroying it with turbolaser fire so that it could not be subjected to closer study. Following the successful launch of the cloaked asteroids, the Imperial forces withdrew. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|For the moment at least, Coruscant is effectively out of the war.|Grand Admiral Thrawn|}} Though Imperial forces had retreated, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was now faced with several major problems. They could not drop the energy shield around the planet for fear that an asteroid might hit the heavily populated cityscape nor could they allow space traffic near the planet in case of collisions. Originally, [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Rieekan]] and Bel Iblis, amongst others, were able to implement a project in which a dust cloud was released from the first transport, before the ''Harrier''s negative ion beams were used to focus it. This dust cloud then would show any asteroids to fly into it, allowing the defenders to destroy it. Although this was effective on the first time, it was later decided to be too time consuming, and so was given up on in favor of acquiring a [[crystal gravfield trap]], a sensor which would detect the cloaked asteroids in orbit around Coruscant. Although Republic ships successfully destroyed all of the asteroids by firing blindly into space, they had no fail-safe way of detecting the asteroids, or of knowing how many asteroids were originally launched and how many remained floating in orbit. As a result, the galactic capital world was effectively blockaded until a method could be devised to detect and eliminate the phantom asteroids. This series of events led to the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]], in which the New Republic attempted to steal a crystal gravfield trap to locate any remaining asteroids in orbit around Coruscant. When Thrawn was assassinated during the battle, the New Republic successfully acquired the gravfield trap and was finally able to lift the quarantine. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|4|Shadows of Darkness|reprint=1}} {{Mo}} *''[[The Last Command]]'' {{Ab|The Last Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[The Last Command 3|''The Last Command'' 3]] {{1stp}} *''[[Before the Storm]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Siege of Coruscant}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *{{FactFile|107|THR 5-6|The Thrawn Campaign}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Thrawn}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht um Coruscant (9 NSY)#legends |es=Asedio de Coruscant }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Coruscant, Siege of}} [[Category:Battles of Coruscant]] [[Category:Battles of the Thrawn campaign]]
